records.ety: [L. prosecutus, p. p. of prosequi to follow, pursue. See Pursue.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Prosecuted (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Prosecuting.]

defs: To follow or pursue with a view to reach, execute, or accomplish; to endeavor to obtain or complete; to carry on; to continue; as, to prosecute a scheme, hope, or claim.
quotes: I am beloved Hermia; Why should not I, then, prosecute my right ?

defs: To seek to obtain by legal process; as, to prosecute a right or a claim in a court of law.

To pursue with the intention of punishing; to accuse of some crime or breach of law, or to pursue for redress or punishment, before a legal tribunal; to proceed against judicially; as, to prosecute a man for trespass, or for a riot.
quotes: To acquit themselves and prosecute their foes.

defs: To institute and carry on a legal prosecution; as, to prosecute for public offenses.
